name: Carril del Dique Trail
name_original: Dique del Molino Caído – running circular desde El Burgo por el Río Turón
description: Jog the easy 2.9-mile (4.7 km) Carril del Dique Trail in Sierra de las Nieves National Park, gaining 128 feet (39 metres) in about 32 minute
region_intro: Jogging routes around El Burgo are set within the Sierra de las Nieves National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offering a diverse natural environment. The region features forests of firs, pines, and oaks, alongside the scenic Turón River basin. Elevated points, including hills over 500 meters, provide panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. This varied terrain makes El Burgo an excellent destination for running amidst natural beauty. Best jogging routes around El Burgo The most popular jogging route is Carril del Dique Trail, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail that takes about 32 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle path along the Dique, suitable for a relaxed run. Another top favourite among local runners is El Burgo Village Center – Molino Caído Dam loop from El Burgo, a difficult 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route explores the village center and leads towards the Molino Caído Dam, offering varied scenery. Local runners also love the Pico del Jarro – Mirador Luis Ceballos loop from Yunquera, a difficult 16.0 miles (25.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Jogging around El Burgo is defined by dense woodlands, river valleys, and panoramic hill views. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in El Burgo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore El Burgo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around El Burgo?
A: El Burgo offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 tours available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging ascents through the Sierra de las Nieves National Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in El Burgo?
A: Jogging routes around El Burgo feature diverse terrain, including dense woodlands, scenic river valleys along the Turón River, and panoramic hill views. You'll find paths through forests of firs, pines, and oaks, with options for both gentle runs and more demanding mountainous sections.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Yes, for an easy and relaxed run, consider the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996250858/carril-del-dique-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carril del Dique Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;. This route is approximately 4.7 km (2.9 miles) long and takes about 32 minutes to complete, offering a gentle path along the Dique.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while running in El Burgo?
A: El Burgo's routes are rich with natural beauty. You can encounter features like the head of the Turón River basin, and enjoy stunning views from elevated points such as the Monument-Mirador al Guarda Forestal. Highlights include the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/613806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla Peak (1,919 m)&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, and the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/719931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Virgen Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;. The region is part of the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, Sierra de las Nieves National Park, ensuring a pristine natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in El Burgo?
A: Yes, many routes in El Burgo are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example of a challenging loop is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21831362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo Village Center – Molino Caído Dam loop from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, which covers about 13.8 km (8.6 miles) and offers varied scenery.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in El Burgo?
A: The running routes in El Burgo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the dense woodlands, river valleys, and panoramic hill views.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, El Burgo offers several difficult routes. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21830784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Jarro – Mirador Luis Ceballos loop from Yunquera&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is a demanding 25.7 km (16.0 miles) trail through mountainous terrain, typically completed in about 4 hours and 9 minutes. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17229015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road loop from Casarabonela&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt;, a difficult 16.8 km (10.4 miles) route.

Q: Can I find routes that offer great viewpoints of the Sierra de las Nieves?
A: Many routes provide stunning vistas of the Sierra de las Nieves. The region is known for its elevated points, including hills over 500 meters high, which offer panoramic views. Look for routes that pass by areas like the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3332090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road section with views of the Sierra de las Nieves&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; or mountain passes such as &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Saucillo&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; for inspiring scenery.

Q: What is the best season for running in El Burgo?
A: El Burgo, located in the Sierra de las Nieves, offers a pleasant climate for outdoor activities. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the spring and autumn months generally provide milder temperatures, ideal for running. Summers can be warm, but early morning or late evening runs are still enjoyable. Winters are cooler but often clear, offering crisp air for invigorating runs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or bridges?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, the region does have historical elements. The Turón River area features the medieval Acequia and the Puente de Málaga (Malaga Bridge). El Burgo itself has three bridges over the River Turón. For a longer, more challenging run, the &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17229015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road loop from Casarabonela&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; hints at historical paths in the wider area.

Q: Are there any routes that explore river valleys or offer waterside running?
A: Yes, the Turón River basin is a prominent natural landmark in the area, offering scenic routes along its banks. Joggers can enjoy the peaceful environment and the sounds of flowing water. The &lt;strong&gt;&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996250858/carril-del-dique-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carril del Dique Trail&lt;/a&gt;&lt;/strong&gt; is an easy option that runs along the Dique, providing a waterside experience.
tips: The Turón river dams are responsible for regulating the flow and controlling excess sediment. From the Molino Caído dam there is a path that goes down to the bathing area, known as Azud Largo.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is approximately 4.7 kilometers long and is rated as easy. For an average jogger, it should take around 30-35 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Carril del Dique Trail is rated as easy and features relatively flat and even terrain. This makes it an excellent choice for beginner joggers or those looking for a relaxed run.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat and even, making it ideal for jogging. You can expect a mix of surfaces, potentially including paved sections, packed dirt, or gravel, as it follows a dike or riverside path.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a notable landmark along the way is the Molino Caído Dam. The trail's waterside location also offers picturesque views of the surrounding landscape and any body of water it borders.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures. Summer mornings or late afternoons can also be good, benefiting from refreshing breezes off the water, but be mindful of the heat during midday.

faqs.question: Is the Carril del Dique Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not detailed, trails in natural areas often permit dogs on a leash. It's always best to keep your dog leashed to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail, especially since it's in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, access to trails within Sierra de las Nieves National Park, including this one, does not require an entrance fee. However, for certain activities or specific areas, permits might be needed. For a standard jog on the Carril del Dique Trail, a permit is typically not required.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Carril del Dique Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is described as a circular route starting from El Burgo. Look for parking options within or near El Burgo, which would provide convenient access to the trailhead for your jog.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers 100% of the 'El Burgo - Ronda' trail and significant portions of 'Carril del Dique - GR 243' (up to 57%). It also touches sections of 'El Burgo - La Fuensanta', 'El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer', 'Camino del Dique', and 'Paseo de la Acequia del Molino'.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while jogging here?
faqs.answer: You can expect scenic views related to the body of water the trail borders, such as reservoir views and surrounding hills. The path offers an open-air running environment, providing a peaceful escape for recreational joggers.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms nearby?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts from El Burgo, you may find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within the town before or after your jog. The trail itself is unlikely to have dedicated facilities directly along the path.
